rulings.Case Title: ROGER HENDERSON VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Realign Defendant-Heir As Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 15, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' and Petitioner's motion for order to realign defendant-heir Brandon Henderson as plaintiff is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 2:15p.m., not 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MELVIN UHR et al VS. AMERICAN LAFRANCE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 15, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under both prongs of Aguilar. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Uhr was exposed to raw asbestos or asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant also sustained its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claim by demonstrating that plaintiffs' exposure allegations pre-date defendant's ownership and operation of its mine and sale of raw asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 2:15p.m., not 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD KEITH ANDERS ET AL VS. AMERICAN BILTRITE INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 15, 2015 in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant The W.W. Henry Company's motion for summary adjudication is denied. As to plaintiff's claim for punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony of Lori Angelo and Paul Beemer and defendant's responses to standard interrogatories create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. As to plaintiff's cause of action for false representation, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting this cause of action. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his false representation cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 2:15p.m., not 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
